grounding_note: Third batch: more third-party-cause and employment-nexus patterns.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm a school custodian who fell through a stage floor and the district's comp process is glacial.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The stage was rebuilt last year by an outside theater-construction company whose subfloor failed inspection after my fall.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: An outside theater-construction company rebuilt that stage last year — their subfloor failed inspection after my fall.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an outside builder's defective work caused the fall
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The stage is original 1970s construction our own maintenance crew maintains.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's original 70s construction our own crew maintains.
scenarios.vetting_note: Recent-outside-work fact creates the construction defendant.

scenarios.opening_query: I was hurt helping unload a truck at my brother-in-law's shop and now nobody wants to pay my ER bill.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Workers' Comp > State || General Litigation > Premises Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: I don't work there — I was doing him a weekend favor, unpaid, so it's his business's premises liability, not a comp claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I don't work there — it was an unpaid weekend favor, so it's on his business's liability side.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He actually pays me cash for Saturdays — I'm on the informal payroll, which the comp system still covers.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He pays me cash for Saturdays — informal payroll, but payroll.
scenarios.vetting_note: Favor-vs-cash-wages: employment existence, not role, is the hidden fact.

scenarios.opening_query: Airbag dust and smoke filled our shop after a customer's car battery exploded next to me. My lungs haven't recovered.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The battery was an aftermarket brand with a known thermal-runaway defect and an open recall.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was an aftermarket battery with a known thermal-runaway defect — open recall and everything.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a defective product caused the exposure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was our own shop charger left on the wrong setting overnight by a coworker.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Our own charger got left on the wrong setting by a coworker.

scenarios.opening_query: My wife slipped in the hotel kitchen where she cooks and shattered her kneecap. The hotel is stalling everything.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The grease trap that overflowed is serviced by an outside company that had skipped two scheduled cleanings — the logs prove it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The outside grease-trap company skipped two scheduled cleanings before the overflow — the logs prove it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an outside service company's failure caused the hazard
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a dish-line spill from the hotel's own short-staffed evening shift.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was a dish-line spill on the hotel's own short-staffed shift.
scenarios.vetting_note: Service-vendor logs give the third-party hook.

scenarios.opening_query: A rideshare driver crashed with me in the back seat and my neck injury is worse than they first thought.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Workers' Comp > Third Party Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was riding for work — my employer books and pays for those rides between our sites, so comp applies alongside the claim against the driver.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was a work ride — my employer books and pays for those trips between sites, so comp sits alongside the driver claim.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the ride was within the course of employment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a Saturday-night personal ride home from dinner.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just a Saturday-night ride home from dinner.
scenarios.vetting_note: Employer-paid-travel nexus; increasingly common fact pattern.

scenarios.opening_query: Our janitorial crew got sick after a night cleaning a client's office building and I'm the worst off.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The client's pest-control contractor had fogged the building that evening and never posted the re-entry warnings.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The client's pest-control contractor fogged the building that evening and never posted re-entry warnings.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether another company's chemical application caused the exposure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was our own crew mixing two cleaning products against the label directions.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Our own crew mixed two products against the label.
scenarios.vetting_note: Multi-employer exposure with a warning-failure defendant.
